collections.name: profiles
collections.description: User profiles containing age, height/weight, ICD-10 diagnosis code

fields.name: string, user name
fields.icd10_code: string, ICD-10 diagnosis code (e.g., E10 = Type 1 Diabetes)

collections.name: sleep_sessions
collections.description: Sleep sessions synced from smart ring. Each record is one night of sleep.

fields.bedtime: datetime (UTC), when the user fell asleep
fields.wake_time: datetime (UTC), when the user woke up
fields.total_sleep_minutes: number, total sleep duration in minutes
fields.time_awake_minutes: number, time spent awake during sleep window
fields.stages: array of {stage: string ('light'|'deep'|'rem'|'awake'), duration_minutes: number, percentage: float}
fields.resting_heart_rate: number, average resting heart rate during sleep (BPM)

collections.notes: bedtime and wake_time are MongoDB datetime objects (UTC). To query 'last night', compute yesterday's local date range and convert to UTC using the user's timezone. Example: yesterday_local = today_local - timedelta(days=1); yesterday_start_utc = datetime(yesterday_local.year, yesterday_local.month, yesterday_local.day, tzinfo=local_tz).astimezone(timezone.utc); yesterday_end_utc = yesterday_start_utc + timedelta(days=1); then filter bedtime $gte yesterday_start_utc $lt yesterday_end_utc.

collections.name: activities
collections.description: Activity records (from smart ring or manual entry)
collections.notes: 13 activity types: walking, running, cycling, swimming, yoga, stretching, dancing, basketball, soccer, tennis, hiking, gym, other

collections.name: walk_tests
collections.description: 6-minute walk test results for assessing cardiopulmonary function

collections.name: daily_steps
collections.description: Daily ring step summaries synced from command 0x51. One document per user per local calendar day from the ring.

fields.date_str: string, YYYY-MM-DD using the ring's local calendar date
fields.steps: number, total steps for that day
fields.exercise_time_seconds: number, active-movement duration in seconds for that day
fields.distance_km: number, total distance for that day in kilometers
fields.synced_at: datetime (UTC), when this daily summary was last upserted

collections.notes: Unique key is effectively (user_id, date_str). Records are upserted, so re-sending the same day overwrites the prior summary. date_str is a plain string, not a Mongo datetime; query ranges with string comparison on YYYY-MM-DD. active minutes should be derived as exercise_time_seconds // 60.

collections.name: hr_readings
collections.description: Single-point heart-rate readings synced from ring history commands (0x55 and detailed 0x54 expansion).

fields.bpm: number, heart rate in beats per minute

collections.notes: Unique key is effectively (user_id, timestamp). Sync uses upsert semantics, so duplicate uploads are safe. This collection does not currently store source or created_at fields.

collections.name: hrv_readings
collections.description: Ring HRV / stress / blood-pressure snapshots synced from command 0x56.

fields.hrv_ms: number, heart rate variability in milliseconds
fields.heart_rate_bpm: number, heart rate captured with the HRV reading
fields.fatigue: number, stress/fatigue score from the ring (0-100)
fields.created_at: datetime (UTC), insert timestamp; unchanged on duplicate upserts

collections.notes: Unique key is effectively (user_id, timestamp). Values are ring-estimated wellness metrics, especially blood pressure; treat them as trend indicators rather than clinical-grade measurements.

collections.name: temperature_readings
collections.description: Ring temperature snapshots synced from command 0x62.

fields.temp1_c: number, first temperature sensor reading in Celsius
fields.temp2_c: number, second temperature sensor reading in Celsius
fields.temp3_c: number, third temperature sensor reading in Celsius

collections.notes: Unique key is effectively (user_id, timestamp). These are ring/skin-adjacent measurements, not oral/core thermometer readings. When summarizing, prefer one representative sensor or report the highest reading explicitly.

collections.name: spo2_readings
collections.description: Ring blood-oxygen saturation snapshots synced from command 0x66.
collections.notes: Unique key is effectively (user_id, timestamp). Sync uses upsert semantics, so duplicate uploads are safe. Isolated low readings may be noisy; trends are usually more meaningful than a single point.

collections.name: tasks
collections.description: Med-Reminder tasks (medication reminders, lifestyle habits, etc.)

fields.team_id: string, optional, used for team tasks
fields.open_datetime: string, start time 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm' (e.g. '2026-03-06 16:00', no Z suffix)
fields.close_datetime: string, end time 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm' (same format as open_datetime)
fields.done: ISODate (MongoDB datetime) if task is completed, field is ABSENT if not completed

collections.notes: CRITICAL — task status is NOT stored. It is computed: (1) done field present → completed; (2) done absent AND close_datetime < now → expired; (3) done absent AND close_datetime >= now → pending. To query by status: completed={"done":{"$exists":true}}, pending={"done":{"$exists":false},"close_datetime":{"$gte":now_str}}, expired={"done":{"$exists":false},"close_datetime":{"$lt":now_str}}. open_datetime/close_datetime are plain strings — filter with string comparison: {"open_datetime":{"$gte":"2026-03-01 00:00","$lt":"2026-04-01 00:00"}}. TIMEZONE CRITICAL — open_datetime and close_datetime are stored in UTC (no Z suffix, but values are UTC). When the question involves 'today', 'now', 'this week', or any local date, you MUST convert using the user's timezone from User Context. Example: if user_timezone='America/Los_Angeles' (UTC-7), then 'today local' maps to a UTC range of today+7h to tomorrow+7h. Always use zoneinfo (Python 3.9+) to compute UTC bounds: from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo; from datetime import datetime, timezone; local_tz = ZoneInfo(user_timezone); today_local = datetime.now(local_tz).date(); day_start_utc = datetime(today_local.year, today_local.month, today_local.day, tzinfo=local_tz).astimezone(timezone.utc); day_end_utc = day_start_utc + timedelta(days=1); start_str = day_start_utc.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M'); end_str = day_end_utc.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M'); then query open_datetime $gte start_str $lt end_str. Never assume UTC==local time.

collections.name: task_templates
collections.description: Reusable task templates for batch task generation

collections.name: teams
collections.description: Teams connecting teens with parents, coaches, or caregivers
collections.notes: Soft-deleted teams have is_deleted=true. Always filter {is_deleted: {$ne: true}} unless specifically querying deleted teams.

collections.name: team_members
collections.description: Team membership records. Tracks both active members and pending invitations for registered users.

fields.invited_by: string, user_id of the person who sent the invite

collections.notes: Only status='member' records count as active team members. status='pending' means the user was invited but hasn't accepted yet. Pending members have no access to team data.

collections.name: pending_invitations
collections.description: Email invitations sent to users who are not yet registered in the system
collections.notes: These are for unregistered users only. Registered users get a team_members record with status='pending' instead.

collections.name: dayprints
collections.description: Daily action logs — a per-user, per-day record of notable events (task completions, advisor interactions, insights)

fields.events: array of {event_id: string, type: string ('task_completed'|'advisor_chat'|'important_insight'), timestamp: datetime, data: object (varies by type)}

collections.notes: One document per user per day. The 'date' field is UTC date string. Query by user_id + date range to get a user's daily activity log.

collections.name: advisor_checkins
collections.description: Per-user Advisor Proactive Mode preferences. When enabled, the daemon periodically calls the proactive advisor service which fetches user data and asks Claude Haiku whether to send a nudge.

fields.enabled: boolean — whether proactive mode is on
fields.last_sent_at: string, ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last proactive check run (regardless of whether a nudge was sent)
